bull.t: Very Strong Balance Sheet And Equity Growth
bull.b: Minimal leverage and rapidly expanding equity provide durable financial flexibility: supports higher R&D/CapEx, M&A (Prolec), and working-capital swings without forcing distress financing. A strong capital base materially reduces refinancing and solvency risk over the next 2–6 months.

A rising, multi-year backlog and strong book-to-bill create durable revenue visibility and capacity utilization predictability. This pipeline underpins equipment and services demand, smooths near-term revenue volatility, and supports sustained margin and cash conversion through 2026.

bull.t: Power And Electrification Margin Expansion And Pricing Power
bull.b: Improving segment margins and ability to secure higher $/kW pricing on gas power orders reflect structural pricing power and operational leverage. Sustained higher margins in Power and Electrification meaningfully improve free cash flow generation and earnings durability over the medium term.

bear.b: Large, ongoing Wind losses materially weigh on consolidated profitability and cash generation. Structural issues (lower deliveries, tariff exposure, and capacity timing) could require sustained remediation, keeping volatility in consolidated margins for multiple quarters.

Tariffs are a structural regulatory cost that can persist across quarters, eroding competitiveness and margins if not offset by pricing or alternate sourcing. The $250–350M hit for 2026 is large enough to reduce near-term returns and complicate project economics across impacted markets.

bear.t: Variable Cash Conversion And One-time Gains
bear.b: Volatility in FCF versus reported earnings and large one‑time M&A gains can obscure core cash generation. If operational cash conversion remains uneven, it raises execution risk for funding capex, integration or returning capital, and reduces visibility on sustainable free cash flow.
